source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Crane Incident

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Green Canyon Block 205

event.top_event: Dropped load - drill line fell from suspended position
actions_taken: Stand down with all crews on board
Contractor developed rig-specific Work Safe Procedure (WSP) and SOP for slipping and cutting drill line
JSA updated to include all necessary steps and risk mitigation
Drill Site Representative (DSR) and HSE Representative assigned to attend JSA discussions and pre-job meetings
Drill line cutting procedure changed to manageable sections placed manually in scrap basket; crane no longer utilized
event.summary: On 13-SEP-2022 at approximately 0035 hours, a 140-foot cut drill line being hoisted by crane on Parker Rig 14 at A-Genesis Spar slipped through a 2-inch nylon strap and fell approximately 80 feet to the catwalk and then to the main deck. No injuries occurred, but personnel were in the area, creating high potential for harm. Root causes included improper rigging (nylon strap too thick to properly secure the drill line), lack of supervision (night WSR not on location), and absence of written procedures for the task.
recommendations: Develop rig-specific Work Safe Procedure (WSP) and Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) for slipping and cutting drill line
Ensure JSA includes all necessary steps and risk mitigation measures
Assign Drill Site Representative (DSR) and HSE Representative to attend all JSA discussions and pre-job meetings
Modify drill line cutting procedure to use manageable sections placed manually in scrap basket
Eliminate crane use in cutting and coiling operations

threats.name: Nylon strap slippage due to improper cinching
Lack of supervision and procedure controls
Personnel positioning in Red Zone under suspended load

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify hazards and establish safe work procedures

supporting_text: A Job Safety Analysis (JSA) was reviewed and signed by all rig crew members involved with this process before work commenced
BSEE noted that the JSA lacked steps pertaining to the process of coiling and disposing of the 148 feet of 1 3/8 inch drill line
Immediately following lunch, the rig crew went directly to the catwalk area and began coiling up the drill line without any discussion or JSA review

controls.name: Proper rigging equipment (nylon strap)
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to secure suspended load

supporting_text: The Derrickman attached a 2-inch nylon strap with 2 wraps and a double half hitch 3 to 4 feet from the tail end of the cut drill line
the drill line slipped through the nylon strap and fell to the catwalk

Documentation provided indicated that the rigging applied to the cut drill line with the 2-inch nylon strap was inadequate. The strap was too wide and thick which prevented the proper cinching/choking on the drill line

controls.name: Well Site Representative (WSR) supervision
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to provide oversight and ensure compliance with procedures

supporting_text: there was no Well Site Representative (WSR) on location during the event. The night WSR went in earlier that day due to no work being planned for the evening
Supervision: No supervision. The night WSR was not on location at the time

controls.name: Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) for drill line cutting and coiling
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to establish standardized safe work methods

supporting_text: there was no Standard Operation Procedure (SOP) in place for slipping and cutting drill line and coiling it up for disposal
Management Systems: No written job procedure. Lack of established methods of performing the task

controls.name: Red Zone identification and personnel exclusion
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to prevent personnel exposure to dropped load hazard
controls.lod_basis: Work area hazard zoning and access control

supporting_text: The rig crew failed to identify the Red Zone area and were working under a suspended load
Personnel working under a suspended load increased the potential for harm

controls.name: All Stop procedure
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to halt operations and prevent escalation

supporting_text: An All Stop was initiated, and rig management was notified followed by a stand down with all crews on board

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
